description_long: This object is a horizontal bar structure commonly found in urban environments such as parks or public spaces. It features a cylindrical wooden top bar supported by two angled metallic posts, with four evenly spaced horizontal metal rods running parallel beneath the top bar. The posts and rods are metallic, likely stainless steel or aluminum, providing durability and resistance to weather. The wooden top bar is smooth and rounded, designed for gripping or leaning. The overall construction is minimalistic and functional, with no visible fasteners or decorative elements. The structure is intended for use as a barrier, railing, or exercise bar.
description: A sturdy horizontal bar with a wooden top and metal supports, featuring four parallel metal rods beneath the main bar. Designed for use as a railing or exercise bar in urban settings.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the wooden top bar, angled metal posts, and four horizontal metal rods.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the narrow profile of the bar, with the wooden top bar appearing as a thin vertical line.
description_view_2: Rear view, visually identical to the front, showing the wooden top bar, angled posts, and horizontal rods.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thin profile of the bar and posts.
